repo_purpose: Shinobi: autonomous Windows-native AI agent for Spanish-language content creators. TypeScript monorepo with local-first memory, desktop automation, web scraping, and optional cloud backend.
architecture_summary:
Shinobi is a TypeScript monorepo targeting Windows-native deployment (Node SEA .exe) for autonomous AI agent workflows. The architecture separates concerns across several top-level directories:

- **src/**: Core application logic including upstream release watchers (hermes_watcher), kill-switch safety controls (PowerShell ESC monitor), permission/path validation, and command execution utilities.
- **scripts/**: Validation, smoke test, and integration runner scripts for /read and /self endpoints, using a lightweight pass/fail pattern rather than a standard test framework.
- **missions/**: Mission configuration directory (content unreadable due to subagent timeout).
- **docs/**, **misc/**: Unreadable during scan.

Key external dependencies include OpenAI SDK, Playwright (web scraping), better-sqlite3 + sqlite-vec (local vector memory), @nut-tree-fork/nut-js (desktop automation), and axios. Build tooling uses esbuild, tsx, ts-node, and a custom build_sea.mjs for single-executable packaging.

The project follows a local-first philosophy with optional cloud backend (OpenGravity). Internal module references from package.json suggest a planned layout (coordinator, tools, cloud, bridge, db, memory, utils, runtime, persistence, skills) though only src/watchers and src/utils were directly observed.

modules.name: watchers
modules.responsibility: Polls GitHub API for Hermes upstream releases, persists state, and notifies CLI on new tags

modules.name: utils
modules.responsibility: System interaction helpers: kill-switch (ESC monitor), permission/path validation, command execution wrapper

modules.name: scripts
modules.responsibility: Smoke tests, integration runners, and diff validation for /read and /self endpoints

modules.name: missions
modules.responsibility: Mission configurations and runtime logic for autonomous agent tasks (content not verified)

risks.description: missions/ directory is unreadable due to repeated 90s subagent timeouts; structure and purpose undetermined
docs/ directory scan unreadable (validation failed twice); documentation structure unverified
misc/ directory scan unreadable (validation failed twice)
package.json declares both ISC and MIT licenses, creating license ambiguity
Test script in package.json prints 'no test specified' despite existence of test_b*.ts and __tests__ files; no standardized framework (jest/vitest/mocha)
runner.ts executes commands via execSync without invoking permissions.isDangerousCommand(); dangerous-command guard exists but is unused, creating a security gap
scripts/a4_run.ts contains hardcoded absolute Windows paths (C:\\Users\\angel\\...) that break portability
hermes_watcher.ts updates last_check_iso even on API failures, potentially masking outage intervals
kill_switch.ts silently leaves proc=null if PowerShell spawn fails, defeating the safety mechanism without notification
permissions.ts WORKSPACE_ROOT falls back to cwd() which may drift at runtime, weakening path-traversal guarantees
runner.ts execSync has no timeout/signal handling; long-running commands block the event loop
a4_run.ts appends to docs/A4_VALIDATION.md without rotation; file grows unbounded
